message:
chore(beads): review-pass-13 — 1 HIGH semver-stability bead (calling it)

Final review pass of this session. Verified via grep before
filing (calibration discipline held).

  frankenpandas-tne4 [HIGH] semver: ZERO #[non_exhaustive] on any
                            public enum across all 12 crates,
                            including 5 pub error enums (FrameError,
                            IoError, ExprError, IndexError,
                            HarnessError). Per Rust semver policy,
                            adding a new variant to a pub enum
                            without #[non_exhaustive] is a MAJOR
                            break. br-fd90 (SQL) + br-niwb (error
                            conformance) + br-7cfm (panics→errs)
                            ALL add variants; without this bead
                            each forces 0.2.0. Fix is one attribute
                            per enum + cargo-semver-checks CI gate.

**Calling the session here.** After 13 review passes:
  2 CRITICAL (d6xa closed)
  40 HIGH (9 closed via swarm)
  5 MEDIUM
  1 LOW
= 48 review beads total, 10 of which have already shipped via
  parallel-agent work during this session.

Every remaining HIGH candidate I could file next has already
been calibrated against and is genuinely MEDIUM:
- Branch protection verification (not in-repo inspectable)
- Golden-file UPDATE_GOLDENS workflow (implicit via existing tests)
- Inline tracing hooks (filed as 7gd4; no sibling HIGH)
- .editorconfig (true but small)
- cargo-machete / cargo-udeps (unused-dep detection; small)
- .gitattributes for line endings (small)

Filing these at HIGH would be padding, not signal. The 48
beads already filed are the real audit output.

Interlock density remaining: ~30 beads still open, forming
~8-10 natural work-clusters (6→CI rewrite · 5→hooks-dir · 4→
docs pipeline · 4→supply-chain · 4→fuzz discipline · 4→perf
pipeline · 7→SQL epic · 6→row-MI follow-ups). Backlog is
structured, not flat.

Workspace 3171/0/13, 0 clippy warnings. Zero shipped-code
bugs across all 13 passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

pandas.read_sql("SELECT * FROM t WHERE id > ? AND kind = ?", con, params=[10, "x"]) -> DataFrame

Current fp-io read_sql always executes query([]) and cannot bind caller-provided parameters. Add params support for positional bindings so filtered reads do not require string interpolation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:06:31.406831398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:19:02.058848232Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:19:02.057071619Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in fp-io; verified via sql_read_with_params tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-tlkc","title":"Add series str.isdecimal unicode-ascii strict conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T01:47:49.460237646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T01:51:19.528241535Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T01:51:19.526461365Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-tne4","title":"[HIGH] semver: zero #[non_exhaustive] on any public enum (incl. 5 error enums) — every new variant after 0.1.0 is a semver break","description":"Semver-stability audit on public error enums.

**Observation.** grep for `#[non_exhaustive]` across all 12 crates: **zero matches**. Meanwhile, the public error-enum surface includes:

  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:142          pub enum ExprError
  fp-index/src/lib.rs:1267        pub enum IndexError
  fp-io/src/lib.rs:23             pub enum IoError
  fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:2890  pub enum HarnessError
  fp-frame/src/lib.rs:26          pub enum FrameError

plus these crates' total public-surface type counts:
  fp-types:    7 pub enum|struct
  fp-expr:     6 pub enum|struct
  fp-io:       11 pub enum|struct
  fp-frame:    35 pub enum|struct

(12-crate total substantially higher — these four are samples.)

**Semver implication.**
- Per Rust's semver policy (codified by cargo-semver-checks + https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/semver.html), adding a new variant to a `pub enum` without `#[non_exhaustive]` is a MAJOR version break.
- Downstream users who `match` on the enum with an exhaustive pattern compile-break when the enum grows. Users who match with `_ => ...` don't break, but not all users do.
- Error enums in an active library grow constantly: new IO formats → new IoError variants, new expr syntax → new ExprError variants, etc. Our swarm's velocity makes additions almost guaranteed over any multi-week window.
- Without `#[non_exhaustive]`, every single variant addition after v0.1.0 forces bumping to v0.2.0 — rapidly, with breaking implications for anyone pinned to 0.1.x.

**Concrete upcoming breaks:**
- br-frankenpandas-fd90 (SQL backend epic) will add variants to IoError for PostgreSQL/MySQL backend errors. Without #[non_exhaustive]: semver break.
- br-frankenpandas-1zzp (row MultiIndex, closed) already added variants to FrameError for row-MI validation. If 0.1.0 had shipped before, 1zzp would have forced 0.2.0.
- br-frankenpandas-niwb (error-path conformance) will add ~20 variants across IoError + FrameError + ExprError to carry pandas-matched category tags. Without #[non_exhaustive]: semver break per family.
- br-frankenpandas-7cfm (#Panics: contract) might promote some panics to Err(SomeVariant); each new variant → break.

**Severity.** HIGH — semver-compliance gap that is almost free to fix today (add one attribute per error enum) but expensive to retrofit after 0.1.0 ships. br-4clx (release workflow) is the deadline: whatever non-exhaustive story ships in 0.1.0 is the contract we're stuck with.

**What `#[non_exhaustive]` does.**
```rust
#[non_exhaustive]
pub enum IoError {
    Parse(String),
    Io(io::Error),
    // ... existing variants ...
}
```
Downstream users matching on IoError:
```rust
match err {
    IoError::Parse(s) => ...,
    IoError::Io(e) => ...,
    // Forced: must have a _ arm because enum is non_exhaustive.
    _ => panic!("unknown IoError variant"),
}
```
The `_` arm is the seat belt that lets us add Parse2 / Io2 / NewVariant without breaking downstream.

**Fix shape:**

1. Add `#[non_exhaustive]` to EVERY public enum in fp-* crates where:
   - The enum is a pub error type. (Mandatory.)
   - The enum represents a config option, opt-set, kind-tag, or anything that might grow. (Recommended — add unless the enum is genuinely closed, like an enum of 4 known states that can never be extended.)

2. Audit pub structs too. `#[non_exhaustive]` on structs prevents construction-by-literal from outside the crate but allows .field access. Apply to config structs (CsvReadOptions, ExcelReadOptions, ExcelWriteOptions, JsonOrient variants, etc.) where new fields are likely. Don't apply to small value-type structs (Scalar, IndexLabel) that users might legitimately construct via literal.

3. CI gate: add `cargo-semver-checks` to CI (as its own job, cheap). On every PR, semver-checks compares the crate's public API vs the last published version (once br-4clx releases). Flags breaking changes before they ship.

4. Document in CONTRIBUTING.md: "all new pub enums start with `#[non_exhaustive]`; remove the attribute only when the enum's variants are provably closed and stable."

5. README capability-table or a new SEMVER.md: explain the `#[non_exhaustive]` policy so downstream users know why they need a `_` match arm.

Blast radius:
- ~15-25 enum annotations across the workspace (~30 lines total).
- ~20-40 struct annotations if we also apply to config structs.
- 1 new CI job (cargo-semver-checks install + run, ~15 lines in ci.yml).
- CONTRIBUTING.md + SEMVER.md additions (~30 lines).

Zero source-logic changes. Attribute-only diff + CI wiring.

Prerequisites:
- None to start. Attribute additions can land today.
- br-4clx (release workflow) — first release MUST have the correct non_exhaustive story; coordinate timing.
- br-ffhs (CI job splitting) — cargo-semver-checks becomes the 6th parallel CI job in the refactor.

**Related:**
- br-4clx (release + semver policy) — this bead operationalizes the policy.
- br-niwb (error-path conformance) — error-variant adds naturally happen in niwb's PRs; non_exhaustive prevents those from being semver breaks.
- br-fd90 (SQL backend epic) — slice 1's SqlConnection trait (landed) is the one non_exhaustive attribute that MIGHT already exist; the rest needs audit.
- br-7cfm (#Panics: docs) — a pub fn that previously panicked now returning Err adds a variant; non_exhaustive makes that non-breaking.
- br-liai (differential fuzz) — when a fuzz-discovered bug triggers "add a specific Err variant to reject this input," non_exhaustive means the fix is a minor version bump not a major.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:34:03.744321423Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:34:03.744321423Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api-stability","release","review","semver"]}
